title: GTCX AI CODEOWNER Review
description: Schema for structured review output produced by the dual-AI CODEOWNER bot. Every PR review against a code-owned path produces exactly one document conforming to this schema.

schemaVersion.description: Pin to v1.0.0. Changes require ADR + downstream consumer notification.

account.description: GitHub account performing the review (e.g. gtcx-agent)

provider.description: Which AI provider produced this review. Captured so audit trails can correlate review quality with provider — and surface fallback events when Anthropic is unavailable and OpenAI took over.

actionVersion.description: Version of gtcx-codeowner-action that produced this review

playbookVersions.description: Map of playbook name to git SHA of the playbook file at review time

decision.description: APPROVE is intentionally absent. The bot must never approve a PR. A human CODEOWNER retains sole approval authority. If no findings warrant change, the bot still emits COMMENT with an empty findings array and a summary noting the review pass.

playbooksApplied.description: Which playbook(s) were applied based on changed file paths.

summary.description: Human-readable summary posted as the top-level review comment. Should reference finding count by severity and call out the highest-severity finding inline.

metrics.description: Optional self-reported metrics for the review-log dashboard.

id.description: Per-review finding ID, F-0001 onward, zero-padded.

title.description: One-line title in the form '<verb> <object>'. Example: 'Custom block-cipher implementation introduced'.

line.description: Start line of the offending region in the PR head.

endLine.description: Optional end line for multi-line regions.

snippet.description: Verbatim excerpt from the diff, max 500 chars.

playbookRef.description: Reference to the playbook check that surfaced this finding, e.g. 'crypto#3'.

rationale.description: Why this is a finding. Must reference the playbook rule and the specific evidence in the diff. Avoid speculation.

recommendation.description: Concrete fix or mitigation. Should reference an exact alternative pattern or file.

references.description: Links to ADRs, threat-model entries, RFCs, NIST publications, etc.

confidence.description: How confident the bot is that this is a true finding. Findings with confidence=low must include rationale explaining the uncertainty.
